note: Derived from labelled synthetic fixtures via scripts/build_analysis.py. Every series carries evidence_class 'synthetic_test_fixture'; none is a published statistic. Missing periods are reported as gaps and are never counted as zero.

limitations: No baseline is defined for this series, so no deviation from baseline is published.
source_limitations: Generated by scripts/generate_synthetic_fixtures.py. This value is a synthetic test fixture, not a published statistic, and must not be read as an observation of the real world.
This is a market benchmark for a third route, used only as a directional indicator. It is not a Thailand shipment quotation, not a Thailand average, and not a rate any shipper was charged.
No qualified dataset covering Thailand-origin freight rates exists in this registry, so no Thailand freight average is published anywhere in the platform.

indicators.baseline_definition: Zero, defined by the publisher as the series average expressed in standard deviations. Deviation is publishable for this series because the baseline is explicit and published.
source_limitations: Generated by scripts/generate_synthetic_fixtures.py. This value is a synthetic test fixture, not a published statistic, and must not be read as an observation of the real world.
A global index cannot establish a Thailand-specific or lane-specific conclusion on its own.

A retail pump price is domestic cost context. It is not a bunker fuel price and not a cost any carrier actually pays for ocean freight.
